I love the aluminum feel of the body, and the size of it is nice and compact, but right away when charging the phone and setting it up it overheated to a point that felt dangerous. And it runs on an old android OS with absolutely no level of upgrade. So I returned it.

Update:
After using this phone for a few weeks without any major drops it began to fall apart. While I wasn't extremely easy on this case I also wasn't particularly rough with it and was disappointed that it didn't last longer. The phone was not damaged as the case fell apart, but it no longer looks nice and doesn't protect the upper right corner of the phone where parts of the case have snapped off leaving the phone exposed to damage.

This is a great looking and nice quality case. I haven’t dropped it yet, so I can’t say for sure that it holds up well to trauma, but it certainly looks and feels like it offers great protection.

The black and white design is really classy and elegant looking, and goes well with my rose gold iphone 6S. I think it would look good with almost any color, but the pink combined with the black and white looks especially nice in my opinion.

Protection:
To be totally honest I have no idea what the case is made of (plastic? silicone?) and while I am sure  I could find out I don’t really care especially. It combines hard and soft materials to provide both cushioning and a hard shell, which I think is a very effective design. It has a raised bumper that protects the front of the phone when it is laid onto a hard surface, and unlike many other bumper cases this one is really substantial without being bulking. If you set your phone face down on a table, for example, the screen does not touch at all, but very little bulk is added to the phone.

Quality:
I could look into exactly what the case is made of and comment on that and how durable it is or isn’t, but the fact is it just looks and feels like good quality. I have another case that is flimsy cheap plastic, and the difference between the two is evident at a glance. The quality of the design is also good, so it really is a nice looking case that stands out.

Use:
The volume and power buttons are covered but still easy to use, and all of the ports/docks are easy to access while the case is on the phone. I have not yet found any interruption to wifi or bluetooth signals from the case, and the camera is unaffected by it. Both ends of the case have softer flexible material that makes it very easy to get on and off while still providing a very snug, seamless fit.

Appearance:
The black and white stripes sort of make me think of a circus tent, but not a tacky one. It is a distinct and class looking case that will stand out, but since it is black and white it is still versatile enough to go with everything (more or less). The material has a slight gloss to it, making it really pop.

I am really overall very pleased with this phone. I don’t think you can get much better protection without switching to something like an otterbox or something that fully encases your phone, and I really love the way it looks. Since for me, and most people I know, my cell phone has become a constant accessory it is nice to be able to dress it up a little with this cute case while still feeling confident it is protected if I drop it.

I received this product for a discounted price in exchange for my honest review. The discount is not contingent upon a positive review, and my reviews are an honest account of my experiences with and opinions of the products I receive.

I'm really glad there were 3 protectors in this package because it took three tries to get one positioned precisely over the screen of the phone AND with no bubbles and (just a few) dust particles. And this is remarkable for me because I am really good at applying these protectors perfectly in one try (so good that EVERYONE asks me to apply their protectors). But this brand was not easy and ultimately it's not acceptable coverage of the flat part of the phone screen.

**Be forewarned: despite what they package claims, there are NO instructions included. The video the company posted on youtube just shows the product and what's in the package - it does NOT explain how to apply the protectors. (Which is really weird - this company needs some social media help asap!)

If you've used screen protectors before, you'll figure it out. If you haven't - if this is your first time applying a screen protector - you should go with a different product that has clear instructions and an application video to help you.

One thing I originally liked about these protectors versus TechArmor (which is what I've used in the past) is that you don't have to do the awkward and slow-peel-and-roll-the-first layer-into-place-one-little-bit-at-a-time routine... which is a major pain. Instead, with these protectors you peel off the first cover and just apply the entire thing at once (It's more like a hard shell than a flexible piece of plastic film at this first stage). Then you press out the bubbles and remove the second layer of plastic "mask" just like any other protector.

Although I liked this "easier" application, I just could not get one of these positioned properly until the third try - and even then it wasn't right. The biggest problem is that these are barely wide enough to cover the black part of the screen, and you have to make sure you get it positioned exactly right or you won't cover the entire black screen part - and besides being inadequate protection, it shows up really noticeably that it's off kilter and looks bad. But the more you keep re-positioning it to get it perfectly covered, the more dust can get in and settle. This is really annoying - and ultimately this was not an "easier" application.

There is still a really large sliver of phone that isn't covered by the protector OR the lip of my phone case, so I'll be looking for another option asap. These phones are way too expensive to not be able to cover the entire screen and sides when using both a protector and case.

Yes, yes, I know that all of the protectors on the market stop short of the edge of the new iphones due to the curved face of the phone; however this brand of protectors stop WAY short of the sides. These are much shorter than others I've seen on my friends' phones - so you have to really be sure you position these perfectly for best protection and looks. Unfortunately they don't come with any kind of markings or sight guide to get it lined up properly and you have the problems I mentioned above. This wouldn't be an issue if they were wider and a bit more "forgiving" about line up.

Overall, I don't see anything remarkable or extraordinary about this protector once it is on the phone. It is clear but very smudgy - which is just like every other "clear" protector on the market. You'll need to keep a microfiber cloth handy for frequent cleaning if you want it to actually be "clear" rather than smudgy and smeared. The bare phone is so much neater than the screen protectors are.

This package does come with a lint free piece of cloth for cleaning, but you'll need to use your credit card or license to smooth out bubbles and you'll need your own tape to pick up stray dust particles during the application. Other brands include these in the package for the same price (if that's important to you - I don't really care).

There is really nothing special about these protectors.I had hoped these would be better quality than other screen protectors I've used, but I was disappointed - particularly by the large amount of the flat screen / face of the phone that is still exposed after applying the protector and my iphone case.

Yes, this braided cable looks like a solid MFI cable but after several months it simply does not reliably charge newer devices and eventually won’t charge things like an iPad Pro at all. Looks can be deceiving. This is the third fact-looking, high-rated, and overpriced braided lightening cable that has turned to junk, 2/3 I’ve thrown in the trash and is headed there there soon. Add a splitter in the mix and it won’t work at all. It’s as, because if still looks new. It’s just a piece of junk.

In contrast, my all-vinyl Belkin cable that is way more barebones and un-sexy that’s twice as old and was cheaper has held up flawlessly over the years. It looks cheap but it hasn’t fallen apart like the Apple stock cables but is about the same guage.

In short, don’t let the braiding fool you. It often hides a garbage cable inside. If it gets super hit when charging your device, that means it won’t be long before it fails and you should send it back. It means it’s krapp! The Undead cables are the exception, I am sure there are more. Just read the one and two star reviews. There is where you’ll get the real story!

I found these tools to be very poorly made and designed. I haven't been this disappointed in an item in quite a while. While this product looks and sounds great it is not. When they come out it is almost impossible to try to get them back in their correct spots because the lettering on everything is so small. Even the instructions are in such small print that even a person with 20/10 vision would be hard pressed to read it without the use of some magnification. As I carried it around I found out that the bits just would fall out even when locked in the down position. The plastic pieces were cheaply made and didn't last long. The metal tools were better but you can only use them on a few things. The Anti Static strap was the only good thing in this kit and it could use a longer cord and a way to plug it into the ground of an electrical outlet and not just clip onto a chassis or grounded object. My biggest issue was with the screw driver set because even the screw driver itself was difficult to grip to get any torque at all. It was almost impossible to use this tool kit to remove the screws from an IP Camera that should have been simple. I was really hoping for a good long lasting tool kit out of this and instead I got something I will end up throwing away because I can keep the bits in the correct slot because of a horribly designed plastic case and terrible instructions. Avoid this like the plague in my opinion. I am still attempting to find the perfect set of tools to work on all mobile phones/Xbox/Controllers/IP Cameras and other small electronics and am not having much luck!

I'm not brave enough to fully submerge my iPhone in water to test this case, but I did test it several times with various proxies, which did come out dry. When I put a paper towel (chosen largely at random, but also because it seemed a small amount of water would be immediately obvious) inside the case and held it under a tap, the paper towel was still dry when I took it out, which is very promising. I even ran water over the top of the case, where it closes, and the seal held, not allowing any water to penetrate to my paper towel cell phone stand-in. I repeated the test with the paper instruction card that came with the case with a similarly promising result, but I'm still just not ready to dunk my very expensive phone.

In addition to its effectiveness in my kitchen-lab product test, I was also very happy that my phone fits into this case while still inside the bulky Otter Box case I already use. This is perfect because it's much more convenient to stick both the phone and the case into the second case for added protection and water proofing than to switch cases.

The reason I wanted to try this case is not because I plan to start submerging my phone in water, but because I spend a lot of time at pools as a caregiver and really need to have my phone on me. I wanted something that would keep my phone safe from getting wet if it were splashed, if I accidentally set it down on a wet chair, or if I need to pick it up with wet hands. I'm also terrified that I will actually just drop it into the pool because I drop it all the time, hence the Otter Box defender case. It's great that I can keep the protection my phone already has and quickly add this over top of it as a back up.

Update:
After using the case for a while I took several stars off my rating. First, while initially it seemed that the case wasn't affecting the usability of my phone, after using it properly (**trying to use it**) several times at the pool, I've realized that it's nearly impossible to type or do anything but the most basic functions with the phone in the case. You can see the screen okay, so reading things on the phone is possible. As for how water proof it is... I don't trust it that much. The top part actually fell apart apart, and while all the pieces just sort of clipped back together, I really wouldn't trust submerging it without retesting it each time. However, I am still using it. I stick my phone in here while I'm at the pool and only take it out occasionally, and feel more comfortable if it gets splashed. That's splashed with a few drops of water, not dunked or soaked.

In order to keep the iPhone from going to sleep while viewing I needed to turn off display auto-shutoff completely since IOS apparently has no VR helper features to speak of. The phone would get so hot that it made me sweat which fogged up the viewer. I had gotten these goggles for the sole purpose of watching movies in a VR-capable player as well as YouTube, and while the image quality was okay (once I put the notch of the goggles uncomfortably down the bridge of my nose), I found that basic controls (even play and pause) didn't work properly and the experience was incredibly frustrating. I was excited at the idea of having Alexa voice integrated in my phone, I thought it would be a great substitute as Samsung‘s Bixby sucks and I’m not a fan of Siri. Unfortunately the voice only doesn’t work very often, it has a lot of bloatware, and for some reason won’t upgrade the OS. I ended up returning this and purchasing the Motorola Z4 from Best Buy so that it would be completely unlocked and without extra unnecessary software.
Alexa aside, Motorola provide some superb quality phones are amazingly low prices. What Samsung would charge you $1000 for, Motorola charges you for hundred dollars with the same features. I can’t believe more people don’t use Motorola phones.

Let me explain, curved phones will always need non-glass screen protectors. I don’t care what you say there is no tempered glass screen protector that fits well on a curved
phone. All the companies have tried it, and I have tried most of them, and it’s always a mess and I end up having to replace it pretty quickly. So I am happy to use a screen protector that will be flush to the screen, and that will allow me to use the fingerprint sensor on the screen which doesn’t work as well with glass. This brand is always cost effective and reliable. And it did not let me down with this item either

First off it's unsturdy and can be knocked over very easily. Secondly, when you pull it up to the height that you want, there's no way for you to lock it in place so that it doesn't start to slip back down. Thirdly, the part that holds your phone is hard to fit your phone into, it needs to have a bigger opening to make it easier for you to slide your phone into so you can lock it in. Fourthly, it claims that you can use the controller while you're using your phone with your social media sites, this is not true. You have to make your own videos using your phone and then you can upload them to your social media sites, however this doesn't work out properly with most sites except for YouTube or posting videos on your Facebook, things that you aren't planning on editing. The only real cool thing about this is that it does have lots of bright colors and bright variations of white light, but sometimes I like to play around with the lights and it's hard to see them on the videos. Lastly the little thing that you screw into the ring that connects to the part that holds your phone is cool because it's got 3 different spots that you can choose from however my issue with this is that they should supply you with extra parts so you can use them to hold your digital camera or another phone or what would really make it awesome is if it had a little mirror.
